1.arabic: بسم الله الرحمن الرحيم عم يتساءلون
1.english: About what are they asking one another?
About the great news -
That over which they are in disagreement.
No! They are going to know.
Then, no! They are going to know.
Have We not made the earth a resting place?
And the mountains as stakes?
And We created you in pairs
And made your sleep [a means for] rest
And made the night as clothing
And made the day for livelihood
And constructed above you seven strong [heavens]
1.transliteration: Wa banaina fauqakum sab 'an shi daada

1.english: And made [therein] a burning lamp
And sent down, from the rain clouds, pouring water
1.transliteration: Wa anzalna minal m'usiraati maa-an saj-jaaja

1.english: That We may bring forth thereby grain and vegetation
1.transliteration: Linukh rija bihee habbaw wana baata

1.english: And gardens of entwined growth.
Indeed, the Day of Judgement is an appointed time -

1.arabic: يوم ينفخ في الصور فتأتون أفواجا
1.english: The Day the Horn is blown and you will come forth in multitudes
1.transliteration: Yauma yun fakhu fis-soori fataa toona afwaaja

1.english: And the heaven is opened and will become gateways
1.transliteration: Wa futiha tis samaa-u fakaanat abwaaba

1.english: And the mountains are removed and will be [but] a mirage.
1.transliteration: Wa suyyi raatil jibaalu fa kaanat saraaba

1.english: Indeed, Hell has been lying in wait
For the transgressors, a place of return,
In which they will remain for ages [unending].

1.arabic: لا يذوقون فيها بردا ولا شرابا
1.english: They will not taste therein [any] coolness or drink
1.transliteration: Laa ya zooqoona feeha bar daw walaa sharaaba

1.english: Except scalding water and [foul] purulence -
An appropriate recompense.
Indeed, they were not expecting an account
And denied Our verses with [emphatic] denial.
1.transliteration: Wa kazzabu bi aayaa tina kizzaba

1.english: But all things We have enumerated in writing.
1.transliteration: Wa kulla shai-in ahsai naahu kitaa ba

1.english: "So taste [the penalty], and never will We increase you except in torment."
1.transliteration: Fa zooqoo falan-nazee dakum ill-laa azaaba

1.english: Indeed, for the righteous is attainment -
Gardens and grapevines
And full-breasted [companions] of equal age
And a full cup.

1.arabic: لا يسمعون فيها لغوا ولا كذابا
1.english: No ill speech will they hear therein or any falsehood -
1.transliteration: Laa yasma'oona fiha lagh waw walaa kizzaba

1.english: [As] reward from your Lord, [a generous] gift [made due by] account,

1.arabic: رب السماوات والأرض وما بينهما الرحمن ۖ لا يملكون منه خطابا
1.english: [From] the Lord of the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them, the Most Merciful. They possess not from Him [authority for] speech.
1.transliteration: Rabbis samaa waati wal ardi wa maa baina humar rahmaani laa yam likoona minhu khi taaba

1.arabic: يوم يقوم الروح والملائكة صفا ۖ لا يتكلمون إلا من أذن له الرحمن وقال صوابا
1.english: The Day that the Spirit and the angels will stand in rows, they will not speak except for one whom the Most Merciful permits, and he will say what is correct.
1.transliteration: Yauma yaqoo mur roohu wal malaa-ikatu saf-fal laa yata kalla moona illa man azina lahur rahmaanu wa qaala sawaaba

1.arabic: ذلك اليوم الحق ۖ فمن شاء اتخذ إلى ربه مآبا
1.english: That is the True Day; so he who wills may take to his Lord a [way of] return.
1.transliteration: Zaalikal yaumul haqqu faman shaa-at ta khaaza ill-laa rabbihi ma-aaba

1.arabic: إنا أنذرناكم عذابا قريبا يوم ينظر المرء ما قدمت يداه ويقول الكافر يا ليتني كنت ترابا
1.english: Indeed, We have warned you of a near punishment on the Day when a man will observe what his hands have put forth and the disbeliever will say, "Oh, I wish that I were dust!"
1.transliteration: In naa anzar naakum azaaban qareebaiy-yauma yan zurul marr-u maa qaddamat yadaahu wa ya qoolul-kaafiru yaa lai tanee kuntu turaaba
